vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1579: Drop ABI-locked better-sqlite3 in favor of an ABI-stable SQLite (node:sqlite / bun:sqlite)

plan.title: Drop ABI-locked better-sqlite3 in favor of an ABI-stable SQLite (node:sqlite / bun:sqlite)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: better-sqlite3 is a V8-ABI native addon whose compiled .node binary is locked to one Node major. Under npx the prebuilt binary is cached per package version, not per Node version, so a Node major change yields ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED (NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch) at the first new Database(). The shipped self-heal (native-sqlite-guard.ts) shells out to npm rebuild and re-execs — a band-aid that depends on network and a writable install dir.
narratives.Proposal: Move the synchronous SQLite layer behind ONE shared adapter (src/lib/database/driver.ts: openDatabase(path) + SqliteDatabase/SqliteStatement types) that selects bun:sqlite under Bun and node:sqlite under Node — both runtime-bundled and ABI-proof. The adapter shims the two methods node:sqlite lacks (pragma(), transaction() with savepoint nesting) and suppresses the SQLite ExperimentalWarning. Migrate all 8 runtime DB-open sites and all type-only imports to the adapter, delete native-sqlite-guard.ts, then drop better-sqlite3 + @types/better-sqlite3 and bump engines to >=22.5.0. Verified on Node 22.22: node:sqlite loads flag-free, bundles FTS5, has iterate(), accepts Float32Array/Buffer binds, returns BLOB as Uint8Array (existing read code already compatible).

autoDecisions.rationale: Issue lists node:sqlite as the primary path; it keeps the existing synchronous run/get/all surface (WASM is async/bigger API change with perf cost) and pairs naturally with bun:sqlite behind one adapter, both runtime-bundled. Verified FTS5, iterate(), and BLOB round-trip work on Node 22.22.

node:sqlite shipped in 22.5.0 and loads flag-free on the verified dev Node (22.22). The issue acceptance criterion says >=22.5. Docs recommend 22.16+/24 for stability without forcing a Node-24-only floor on users.

autoDecisions.summary: transaction() implemented uniformly in the adapter for both runtimes instead of delegating to bun:sqlite's native transaction.
autoDecisions.rationale: Guarantees identical commit/rollback/nesting semantics regardless of runtime and keeps a single tested code path; node:sqlite has no transaction() at all, so a shim is required for Node anyway.

autoDecisions.summary: Do NOT add --experimental-sqlite to launch paths; suppress only the SQLite ExperimentalWarning in-process.
autoDecisions.rationale: Flag is not required on the verified Node and adding it across bin shebang/pty-supervisor/launchers is broad and fragile. A tightly-scoped process.emitWarning filter removes the log noise without hiding other warnings; early-22.5.x flag-required failures get a clear actionable error instead.

items.title: Create shared SQLite driver adapter (driver.ts) with pragma/transaction shims and runtime selection

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/database/driver.ts exporting openDatabase(path: string): SqliteDatabase and the SqliteDatabase / SqliteStatement TypeScript interfaces that all DB code will depend on. Select bun:sqlite when typeof Bun !== 'undefined', else node:sqlite (via createRequire). Present a better-sqlite3-compatible surface: exec(sql), prepare(sql)->{run(...params)->{changes,lastInsertRowid}, get(...params), all(...params), iterate(...params)}, close(). Shim pragma(sql, {simple?}) (exec for sets; prepare(`PRAGMA ${key}`).get() for {simple:true} reads, returning the scalar). Shim transaction(fn)->callable that runs BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK at depth 0 and SAVEPOINT/RELEASE/ROLLBACK TO when nested (track depth on the connection). At module import, install a targeted process.emitWarning filter that swallows ONLY name==='ExperimentalWarning' && /SQLite/.test(message). Wrap the node:sqlite require in try/catch and, on a flag-required error, throw a clear message recommending Node 22.16+/24. The SqliteDatabase type must model BLOB results as Uint8Array. Do NOT auto-coerce booleans. This bead does NOT modify any existing call site.

subItems.title: openDatabase(':memory:') returns a working connection under both Node (node:sqlite) and Bun (bun:sqlite); exec/prepare/run/get/all/iterate/close all function
pragma('journal_mode = WAL') sets and pragma('user_version', {simple:true}) reads back the scalar value correctly

transaction(fn) commits on success, rolls back the whole transaction when fn throws, and a nested transaction inside an outer one uses SAVEPOINT so the inner can roll back while the outer commits — all covered by unit tests using fake timers where any delay is involved
Unit test asserts a raw boolean bind throws (documents the strict-no-coercion contract, H1)
Float32Array blob round-trips: bind a Buffer-of-Float32Array, read the column back as Uint8Array, reconstruct the Float32Array, assert values equal (covers BLOB type + alignment, H4/D6)
Importing driver.ts suppresses the SQLite ExperimentalWarning only; a non-SQLite ExperimentalWarning still emits (asserted in a unit test)
typecheck + lint pass; no import of better-sqlite3 anywhere in driver.ts

items.title: Migrate panopticon.db chokepoint (database/index.ts) to the driver adapter

narrative.Action: Replace getDatabase()'s inline isBunRuntime() branch and require('better-sqlite3') in src/lib/database/index.ts with a single openDatabase(dbPath) call from driver.ts. Swap `import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'` to the adapter's SqliteDatabase type. Preserve all existing pragmas (journal_mode/foreign_keys/synchronous/journal_size_limit), the one-time incremental_vacuum migration, and the 15-minute incremental_vacuum setInterval exactly as-is — they go through the adapter's pragma/exec.

subItems.title: database/index.ts contains no better-sqlite3 import and no isBunRuntime branch; getDatabase() uses openDatabase()
All WAL/foreign_keys/synchronous/journal_size_limit pragmas and the auto_vacuum=INCREMENTAL migration still run and succeed at first getDatabase()
Dashboard server boots and opens panopticon.db cleanly under Node 22 (built dist) with no native rebuild step and no ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED

items.title: Migrate cloister.db (cloister/database.ts) to the driver adapter

narrative.Action: Delete the inline openSqliteDb() helper and its Bun branch in src/lib/cloister/database.ts; open via openDatabase(CLOISTER_DB_PATH). Swap the better-sqlite3 type import to SqliteDatabase. The writeHealthEventsSync transaction (line ~177) must run through the adapter's transaction() shim. Leave the Effect.try wrapper variants (CloisterDatabaseError) untouched.

subItems.title: cloister/database.ts has no better-sqlite3 import and no inline openSqliteDb; uses openDatabase() and the adapter SqliteDatabase type
writeHealthEventsSync batch insert runs in a single transaction via the adapter and a test asserts a mid-batch throw rolls back all rows
Existing cloister/health DB tests pass unchanged (aside from the type import)

items.title: Migrate docs index DB (docs/query.ts + docs/index-builder.ts) to the driver adapter

narrative.Action: Replace the top-level `import Database from 'better-sqlite3'` default imports in src/lib/docs/query.ts and src/lib/docs/index-builder.ts (currently Node-only — would throw under Bun) with openDatabase() from the adapter. These two files share the docs index DB and use FTS5 (verified bundled in node:sqlite), a transaction (index-builder.ts:144), and Float32 embedding blobs. Ensure float32ArrayToBuffer (write) and bufferToFloat32Array (read at index-builder.ts:383) handle Uint8Array results: widen the bufferToFloat32Array param type from Buffer to Uint8Array and keep its dim validation. Verify the FTS5 MATCH query in query.ts works through the adapter.

subItems.title: Neither docs/query.ts nor docs/index-builder.ts imports better-sqlite3; both open via openDatabase()

Docs index build inserts chunks+embeddings inside a transaction and a docs FTS5 MATCH query returns results — exercised by the existing docs index-builder test (migrated off better-sqlite3 in its own bead)
Embedding blob written via float32ArrayToBuffer reads back through bufferToFloat32Array as an equal Float32Array under node:sqlite (Uint8Array result, alignment-safe)

items.title: Migrate artifacts index store (artifacts/index-store.ts) to the driver adapter

narrative.Action: Replace the Node-only require('better-sqlite3') (line ~300) in src/lib/artifacts/index-store.ts with openDatabase(); swap the better-sqlite3 type import to SqliteDatabase. This makes the artifacts index work under Bun where it previously would have thrown.

subItems.title: artifacts/index-store.ts has no better-sqlite3 import; opens via openDatabase() and uses the adapter type
Artifacts index read/write paths typecheck and existing artifacts behavior is unchanged

items.title: Migrate dashboard cache-service DB to the driver adapter

narrative.Action: Replace the require('better-sqlite3') (line ~35) in src/dashboard/server/services/cache-service.ts with openDatabase(); swap the better-sqlite3 type import to SqliteDatabase. Confirm no execSync/event-loop-blocking is introduced (adapter is sync SQLite, which is the existing contract).

subItems.title: cache-service.ts has no better-sqlite3 import; opens via openDatabase() and uses the adapter type
Dashboard server build (npm run build) succeeds and the cache service operates after boot

items.title: Migrate memory FTS DB (memory/fts-db.ts) to the driver adapter

narrative.Action: Replace the require.resolve('better-sqlite3') guard and require('better-sqlite3') (lines ~15/111) in src/lib/memory/fts-db.ts with openDatabase(); swap the better-sqlite3 type import to SqliteDatabase. Memory search uses FTS5 (bundled, verified) and the conditional transaction at line ~266 (db.transaction(execute)()) — that must route through the adapter transaction shim. Remove the now-unnecessary 'run bun install' resolve-failure guard (the adapter owns driver resolution).

subItems.title: memory/fts-db.ts has no better-sqlite3 import or require.resolve guard; opens via openDatabase()
Memory FTS5 search returns results and the conditional transaction path commits/rolls back correctly via the adapter
Existing memory FTS tests pass

items.title: Migrate feature registry storage (registry/feature-registry-storage.ts) to the driver adapter

narrative.Action: Replace the require.resolve('better-sqlite3') check and the better-sqlite3 open in src/lib/registry/feature-registry-storage.ts with openDatabase(); remove the resolve-failure 'run bun install' guard. Preserve the existing pragma usage (3 sites).

subItems.title: feature-registry-storage.ts has no better-sqlite3 import or require.resolve guard; opens via openDatabase()
Registry tag/list/show storage operations typecheck and function unchanged

items.title: Swap residual type-only better-sqlite3 imports/annotations to the adapter type

narrative.Action: Replace all remaining `import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'` and inline `import('better-sqlite3').Database` annotations that were not already swapped by the per-site beads — at minimum src/lib/database/schema.ts, src/dashboard/server/event-store.ts (line ~159 cast), src/lib/database/cost-events-db.ts (lines ~489/505 param annotations) — to the adapter's SqliteDatabase type. Run a final grep to ensure no `from 'better-sqlite3'` / `import('better-sqlite3')` remains in src/ except inside the test-migration and capstone scopes.

subItems.title: grep for `better-sqlite3` across src/ (excluding the 4 test files and package.json) returns no type imports or annotations
npm run typecheck passes with all db params/returns typed via SqliteDatabase/SqliteStatement

items.title: Delete native-sqlite-guard.ts and its two call sites

narrative.Action: Delete src/lib/native-sqlite-guard.ts and remove the ensureNativeSqliteAbi() import + call in src/cli/index.ts (~line 107 import, ~1407 call) and src/dashboard/server/main.ts (~line 10 import, ~58 call). Remove the now-orphaned explanatory comments at those call sites. There is no ABI to heal once the native addon is gone.

subItems.title: native-sqlite-guard.ts no longer exists and no file imports ensureNativeSqliteAbi
CLI and dashboard entrypoints typecheck and start with no orphaned imports or dead comments

items.title: Migrate test files off better-sqlite3 to the adapter / node:sqlite

narrative.Action: Update the three real test imports — src/lib/database/__tests__/pending-auto-merges-schema.test.ts, src/lib/database/__tests__/stuck-schema.test.ts (dynamic import), src/lib/docs/__tests__/index-builder.test.ts — to build in-memory DBs via openDatabase(':memory:') (or node:sqlite directly). Review the command-string fixture in src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/format-tool-input.test.ts that mentions better-sqlite3 and update it to a generic command if it no longer reflects reality. Keep all assertions intact.

subItems.title: No test file imports better-sqlite3; the three schema/docs tests build DBs via openDatabase()/node:sqlite and pass
format-tool-input.test.ts fixture no longer hardcodes a better-sqlite3 require (or is justified to remain) and the test passes

items.title: Remove better-sqlite3 dependency, bump engines, clean build externals and comments (capstone)

narrative.Action: Remove better-sqlite3 and @types/better-sqlite3 from package.json dependencies/devDependencies and bump engines.node to '>=22.5.0'. Remove 'better-sqlite3' from the tsdown external list in src/dashboard/server/tsdown.config.ts (and any root tsdown.config.ts external list). Run bun install to update the lockfile. Update stale comments/doc text that describe better-sqlite3 as the Node driver, and add a one-line note in .claude/rules/dashboard-node22-only.md clarifying that the DB layer is now node:sqlite/bun:sqlite (still Node-22-only for node-pty/circular-ESM reasons, unrelated to SQLite). Final verification across runtimes.

subItems.title: package.json has no better-sqlite3 or @types/better-sqlite3 entry; engines.node is '>=22.5.0'; lockfile updated via bun install
grep -r 'better-sqlite3' src/ returns nothing except incidental historical mentions in unrelated comments; tsdown external lists no longer mention it
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass
Dashboard and CLI start cleanly under Node 22 (built dist), Node 24, and Bun with NO native rebuild step and no ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED — confirming the root-cause fix from the issue acceptance list
